$schema_note: Source of truth for what Keystone builds. Human views: ROADMAP.md (phases) and TASKS.md. Validated by scripts/validate_feature_list.py (run in pytest + CI). Phases 2–5 are aligned to the three compliance layers (build order = phase number; each phase names its layer) per ADR-0011. Each item carries a 'layer' tag. Do not invent scope beyond the roadmap.
rules: id matches KS-NNNN and is unique
id follows KS-0Pnn where P is the phase number
done_criteria is a non-empty list for every item (state how we'll know it's done)
tests must be non-empty once status passes 'planned' (in_progress | done)
every test ref 'path' or 'path::node' must resolve to a real file (and node) in tests/
depends_on (optional) is a list of existing feature ids — a structural prerequisite edge, not just an implied ordering

features.title: src-layout package importable, versioned, typed
features.description: src/keystone/ imports cleanly, exposes __version__, ships py.typed.
done_criteria: import keystone succeeds
keystone.__version__ is a non-empty str
py.typed is shipped in the package

features.title: Console entry point (the front door)
features.description: keystone.__main__:main is the real front door: `keystone demo` (the default) runs the genuine Layer-1 arc offline via keystone.demo.build_run_result and narrates the actual RunResult (keystone.demo.narrate); `keystone version` reports the version. Same real arc as `make demo` / `make ui`.
done_criteria: `keystone demo` (and bare `keystone`) runs the real arc offline and narrates real RunResult fields, exit 0
narration reads actual fields (never fabricated/hardcoded text)
`keystone version` prints a line containing __version__
the installed console script, run as a subprocess, runs the arc offline

features.title: Strict quality gates enforced
features.description: ruff (incl. security S rules), mypy strict, and a blocking coverage floor run as one gate (make check / CI check job).
done_criteria: make check runs ruff + mypy + fast tests + audit and exits 0
coverage below the floor fails the build

features.title: Secret/hygiene hooks enforced in CI
features.description: pre-commit (detect-secrets + hygiene) runs as its own CI gate, not only locally.
done_criteria: ci.yml has a pre-commit job running on push/PR
detect-secrets uses a committed baseline

features.title: Progressive-disclosure docs

done_criteria: CLAUDE.md is <= 120 lines and links to every governance doc
every docs/*.md is reachable and has no broken relative links

features.title: Deterministic-core import boundary enforced

lint-imports passes against the materialized layer packages
the contract runs in pre-commit and CI

features.title: NAT YAML workflow skeleton

done_criteria: a NAT workflow YAML loads/validates
an entry point can resolve and invoke the workflow

features.title: Hash-chained evidence ledger — append
features.description: Append-only SQLite ledger where each entry commits to the prior entry's hash.
done_criteria: appending an event stores a row whose hash includes the previous hash
the first entry chains from a defined genesis value

features.title: Evidence ledger — tamper detection
features.description: Chain verification detects any modification/insertion/deletion.
done_criteria: verify() returns true for an intact chain
verify() returns false when any entry is altered

features.title: Inference config switch (NIM / Ollama)
features.description: One code path; configuration selects hosted NIM (demo) vs local Ollama (production).
done_criteria: config selects the backend without branching the code path
an invalid backend value is rejected with a clear error

features.title: Curated obligation graph with per-node source citations
features.description: ~25–30 obligation nodes spanning EU AI Act Art. 9–15, DORA, India DPDP Act + DPDP Rules 2025, RBI responsible-AI guidance, and PMLA/FIU-IND. Each node carries a source-citation field (instrument + article/section). Re-scoped from the former generic 'compliance agent' (old KS-0201).
done_criteria: the graph has 25–30 nodes covering EU AI Act Art. 9–15, DORA, India DPDP Act + DPDP Rules 2025, RBI responsible-AI, and PMLA/FIU-IND, and every node carries a non-empty source citation (instrument + article/section)

features.title: Deterministic crosswalk/dedup onto a shared control library
features.description: Deterministic mapping that crosswalks and de-duplicates obligations onto a shared control library keyed to ISO/IEC 42001 + FATF + NIST AI RMF. No LLM in this path (deterministic core).
done_criteria: obligations deterministically and reproducibly map onto the shared control library (ISO/IEC 42001 + FATF + NIST AI RMF) with duplicate controls collapsed
every non-empty control_id on an obligation resolves to a real control in the library; an unresolved reference fails the build (ADR-0012 §5b)

features.title: EU hard-law vs India self-certification modality contrast
features.description: The EU hard-law-conformity vs. India self-certification regulatory modality is an explicit, surfaced attribute on the relevant obligations — not buried in prose.
done_criteria: each affected obligation exposes an explicit modality attribute (hard-law-conformity | self-certification) that the output/UI surfaces

features.title: LLM-edge obligation-summary phrasing
features.description: The LLM is used ONLY to phrase human-readable obligation summaries; all graph/crosswalk/modality logic stays in the deterministic core (honors the core/edge boundary).
done_criteria: obligation summaries are produced at the LLM edge while the import-linter boundary keeps graph/crosswalk logic LLM-free

features.title: Citation-validation accuracy budget
features.description: A citation-validation test enforces the accuracy budget: every article/section reference is well-formed and sourced; the build fails on any unsourced or malformed node.
done_criteria: a validation test fails the build if any obligation node has a missing or malformed source citation

features.title: Deterministic deontic-strength guard for edge phrasing
features.description: The deontic strength (binding 'shall/must' vs advisory 'should/may') of an obligation is a deterministic-core fact. KS-0204's edge phrasing is guarded by a tiered (STRONG/MEDIUM/WEAK) classifier cross-checked against enforcement_modality: on any STRONG-strength transition (incl. a STRONG source whose phrasing can't be confirmed) or a HARD_LAW node phrased advisory, phrase_summary falls back to the authoritative curated summary (certainly-faithful over probably-faithful; never fails the build). Within-advisory drift off hard-law is an accepted, chosen scope line. Prevents a drifted verb being shown next to a binding/advisory modality label (KS-0203).
done_criteria: a deterministic tiered (STRONG/MEDIUM/WEAK) classifier in keystone.core, cross-checked against enforcement_modality, detects modal drift; phrase_summary falls back to the curated summary (never raises) on any STRONG-strength transition (including a STRONG source whose phrased force cannot be confirmed) or a HARD_LAW node phrased advisory

features.title: Tool-calling inference seam (complete_with_tools + cross-backend normalization)
features.description: Spike-discovered Phase-3 prerequisite (see the ADR-0011 amendment): plain complete() cannot tool-call, so keystone.llm.inference gains complete_with_tools(messages, tools) -> ToolCallResult. It sends the tools array to whichever backend is active and normalizes the cross-backend tool-call arguments difference the spike found — Ollama returns a JSON object, NIM (OpenAI-style) a JSON string — into one canonical typed shape (arguments ALWAYS a dict). Deterministic LLM-edge infrastructure; NO agent. KS-0301 (the mock agent) calls tools through this seam.
done_criteria: complete_with_tools returns a typed ToolCallResult whose tool-call arguments are ALWAYS a dict, regardless of backend
an Ollama-shaped response (arguments as a JSON object) and a NIM-shaped response (arguments as a JSON string) normalize to an identical result — backend parity
a text-only response yields an empty tool-call list (a valid outcome, not an error); malformed arguments raise a clear, typed error
the fast gate proves normalization with mocked HTTP (no Ollama running, no NIM key); a live tool-call per backend is slow and skips cleanly

features.title: Mock vulnerable payments agent
features.description: A deliberately vulnerable mock payments agent (keystone.assurance) backed by the KS-0300 tool-calling seam, susceptible to indirect prompt injection — the assurance loop's target surface. Vulnerable BY DESIGN: its system prompt + tool-wiring trust the transaction memo as instructions (OWASP ASI / EchoLeak-class), so a memo-injected settlement instruction drives an unauthorized initiate_transfer (a stub that records transfer intent to the evidence ledger). Ships the canonical, typed VulnerabilitySignature + malicious-memo example that the Garak probe (KS-0303) and the L1 fraud fixture (KS-0403) both import.
done_criteria: the mock agent runs end-to-end on synthetic input and is demonstrably susceptible to a defined indirect-prompt-injection vector (memo-instruction injection fires an unauthorized initiate_transfer)

the canonical, typed vulnerability signature + malicious-memo example are one importable source of truth (the L2↔L1 seam), with initiate_transfer a pure stub that only records transfer intent to the ledger

features.title: NeMo Guardrails rails (input/output/dialog)
features.description: A real NeMo Guardrails (v0.22) deterministic input rail wrapping the mock agent, closing the KS-0301 memo-injection hole: it vets the untrusted memo/data field before the agent acts and refuses embedded instructions, while leaving benign and legitimate transfers intact. Uses no main LLM and no embedding model (nothing downloaded). PROVEN by re-running the KS-0303 Garak detector against the guarded agent — the probe that failed 10/12 unguarded reports 0/12 guarded — and a 'vulnerability remediated' finding is written to the evidence ledger. Survives from old KS-0202 (re-phased). Built AFTER KS-0303 (ADR-0011 amendment): the rail's done-criteria needs the detector first.
done_criteria: a real NeMo Guardrails input rail blocks the memo-injection vector (the agent no longer fires an unauthorized initiate_transfer) while benign and legitimate transfers still succeed
re-running the KS-0303 Garak probe against the guarded agent goes clean (10/12 -> 0/12) and a vulnerability-remediated finding is written to the evidence ledger

features.title: Garak red-team as subprocess
features.description: Garak invoked as an isolated subprocess (ADR-0003, not a dependency) against the vulnerable mock agent (KS-0301), with its JSONL report parsed deterministically into typed findings, mapped to OWASP (LLM01 + Agentic ASI) → EU AI Act Art. 15 → India RBI principle, checked for the memo-injection signature, and written to the evidence ledger. Pinned to Garak 0.15.1; fast tests run over captured report fixtures, the live scan is -m slow. Built before KS-0302 so the detector exists before the patch it verifies (ADR-0011 amendment). Survives from old KS-0301 (renumbered within Phase 3).
done_criteria: a garak run is launched via subprocess against the mock agent and its report is captured
the parsed report is mapped to OWASP + EU/India regulation and the memo-injection vulnerability is detected and written to the ledger

features.title: Milestone: end-to-end AI assurance loop
features.description: A milestone-marked test proving the end-to-end Layer-2 loop, ORCHESTRATED BY THE NeMo Agent Toolkit: the existing pieces (KS-0301 agent, KS-0303 Garak detector + OWASP/regulation mapping, KS-0302 Guardrails patch) are composed into one arc — exposed → detected → mapped → patched → verified — each stage writing to the hash-chained evidence ledger. NAT's runtime drives the workflow (keystone_assurance_loop); `make milestone` runs it live (Garak fails 10/12 unguarded -> 0/12 guarded, exploit fires then does not). No new capability; just orchestration. Survives from old KS-0302.
done_criteria: a -m milestone test exercises the full assurance loop and asserts a verifiable, ordered, hash-valid ledger arc (exposed -> detected -> mapped -> patched -> verified)
the loop is orchestrated by the NeMo Agent Toolkit (a registered NAT workflow drives the stages), runnable live via `make milestone`

features.title: Transaction substrate: model + synthetic stream (Layer 1)
features.description: The deterministic Layer-1 data substrate: a typed Pydantic Transaction (id, timestamp, sender/recipient accounts, amount, currency, type, and a free-text memo with untrusted-data semantics) plus a deterministic, seeded synthetic generator. The generator produces mostly-normal traffic with the explicit, opt-in ability to seed FATF-detectable patterns (a structuring / rapid-movement cluster), labelling nothing as fraud. Shaped so the later seam (KS-0403) is possible: the memo is the same injection locus the Layer-2 agent trusted, and the structuring pattern is independently suspicious on financial-crime grounds. No detection (KS-0402) and no seam (KS-0403) yet. Re-scoped within Phase 4: the old monitor/scoring scope moves to KS-0402.
done_criteria: a typed Transaction model round-trips and fails loud on malformed / missing / bad-enum input, and carries an arbitrary free-text memo (the seam locus)
a deterministic seeded generator reproduces an identical stream and can emit a structuring / rapid-movement cluster on explicit request

features.title: FATF typology engine (Layer 1 detection)
features.description: Deterministic, MEMO-BLIND typology detection over the KS-0401 transaction stream: scores structuring / rapid-movement / large-transfer patterns on financial-crime grounds (amounts, timing/velocity, account relationships, named thresholds) and appends verifiable findings to the evidence ledger. Detection NEVER reads the memo field — the orthogonality that keeps the KS-0403 seam's two detections (AML here, prompt-injection in the assurance loop) independent. No LLM (deterministic core). Re-scoped within Phase 4: this is the detection/scoring the old KS-0401 monitor described; the planted-fraud-via-injection content folds into the KS-0403 seam.
done_criteria: the engine scores a synthetic transaction stream for FATF typologies (structuring / rapid-movement) and appends a verifiable finding to the ledger, catching the KS-0401 cluster with zero benign false positives
detection is memo-blind — a test proves identical findings whether the memo field is blank or filled

features.title: L2↔L1 seam milestone (single owned item)
features.description: One owned task asserting the seam structurally: a planted fraudulent transfer enters the Transaction Monitor via the SAME indirect-prompt-injection path Garak flags against the mock payments agent (the memo carrying CANONICAL_MEMO_EXPLOIT / MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE), AND is independently catchable as a FATF typology. The link is asserted structurally, so a refactor that decouples the two fails the build.
done_criteria: a @pytest.mark.milestone test asserts the fraud fixture's injection vector == the Garak-flagged vector and FAILS if a refactor decouples them (structural, not coincidence of two separate fixtures)

the strong binding holds on a shared transaction id: the transfer the memo-blind FATF engine flags == the transfer whose memo resolves to MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE (the single canonical object), with the AML detection proven independent of the memo

features.title: Regulator-format report generation (FINnet primary, goAML second)
features.description: Turn a FATF finding into a regulator-format report with a strict fact/language split: the deterministic core (keystone.core.reporting) assembles ReportFacts (typology, tx ids, amounts, accounts, currency, period, totals, rationale) — the system of record — and the guarded LLM edge phrases ONLY the SAR/STR narrative. A deterministic faithfulness guard (narrative_is_faithful) falls back to an always-faithful template if the narrative introduces or alters any number/id/typology (the same fall-back-not-fail discipline as the deontic guard; human sign-off does NOT replace it). Format-agnostic core: the facts are rendered by adapters — FINnet 2.0 (FIU-IND STR) primary and goAML secondary — modelling known fields with marked placeholders (no fabricated schema). The report is DRAFTED then SIGNED (oversight in the loop) and recorded to the evidence ledger. Phase-4 extension beyond the original KS-0401-0403.
done_criteria: the deterministic core assembles ReportFacts from a finding + transactions and renders them through both a FINnet (primary) and a goAML (secondary) adapter over the same facts

the LLM-edge narrative is fact-faithful: a deterministic guard falls back to the template if the narrative invents or alters a number/id/typology; the report is drafted, signed off, and recorded to the ledger

features.title: Layer-1 milestone (NAT-orchestrated), closing the seam to L2
features.description: A @pytest.mark.milestone test + NAT workflow composing the existing Layer-1 pieces into one end-to-end arc: stream (KS-0401) → memo-blind FATF catches the structuring fraud (KS-0402) → the flagged tx is shown to carry the SAME MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE the Layer-2 loop found & patched (KS-0403 binding; REFERENCED via signature identity + a ledger cross-reference, NOT re-run) → FINnet report drafted with a guarded narrative (KS-0404) → human sign-off → full ordered hash-valid ledger arc. NAT-orchestrated (mirrors KS-0304); a deterministic spine (canned narrative) is fast-gated, the live arc is slow. No new capability — orchestration only. Completes Layer 1 and the three-layer core build. Phase-4 extension beyond KS-0401-0404.
done_criteria: a NAT workflow drives the ingested→detected→seam_bound→reported→signed arc to completion, writing a full ordered hash-valid ledger chain; runnable live via `make layer1-milestone`

the seam stage binds the FATF-flagged transaction id to the same canonical MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE (referenced, not re-run) and a deterministic spine asserts the arc, failing if a stage is missing or out of order

features.title: Demo runner + serializable run-result (the UI's typed contract)
features.description: Composes the KS-0405 Layer-1 milestone arc into a typed, JSON-serializable RunResult that the Phase-5 front-end consumes: the seam transaction, the Layer-1 financial-crime finding (FATF, memo-blind), the Layer-2 AI-security vulnerability (the canonical VulnerabilitySignature + its OWASP/regulatory mapping with EU/India enforcement modality, REFERENCED not re-run), the binding (the shared transaction id + canonical signature), the STR rendered into BOTH FINnet AND goAML (one fact model, two formats — schema v2), and the ordered hash-valid arc/chain summary. Works live (run the arc) and replays from a saved run (save/load JSON). No new detection capability — composition plus a typed VIEW over the system of record (the ledger). The single source the KS-0501/0502/0503 screens render from; the seam-hero redesign's enabling dependency.
done_criteria: the runner composes the Layer-1 arc into a typed, JSON-serializable RunResult carrying the real seam transaction, both findings, and the canonical MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE
the run-result round-trips through save/load (replay): the result loaded from disk equals the live one
every value derives from a real arc run over the seeded synthetic stream — no placeholder/mocked data

features.title: Shared design system + the seam hero screen
features.description: Establish the shared design tokens (palette, type, spacing) as ONE source of truth — both the Streamlit theme (.streamlit/config.toml) and the custom-SVG hero constants draw from it — then build the SEAM HERO: the climax visual where ONE transaction (from the KS-0500 run-result) is shown simultaneously as a Layer-2 AI-security vulnerability (memo prompt-injection — Garak found it, Guardrails patched it) and a Layer-1 financial crime (FATF structuring, flagged memo-blind), BOUND on the shared transaction id and the canonical signature. Custom SVG/HTML embedded in the Streamlit shell and themed to match; renders both live (a KS-0500 run) and from a saved run (replay); degrades honestly on a missing field (clear empty state, never a crash or fake data). The single most important screen in the project. Re-phased from the old 'posture dashboard' KS-0501 (narrative-first Phase-5 redesign).
done_criteria: shared design tokens (palette/type/spacing) are the single source for BOTH the Streamlit theme and the hero SVG (no drift)

the seam hero renders the real seam tx id, both findings, and the canonical signature from a KS-0500 run-result (live + saved replay), making 'one event, two failures' instant and the binding the focal point
a missing run-result field degrades to a clear empty/▮ state, never a crash or fabricated value

features.title: Jurisdiction-contrast hero screen (EU vs India)
features.description: The second hero — the defense is fragmented, and Keystone unifies it. Two contrasts from the KS-0500 run-result, anchored on the same detected risk: (1) ONE RISK, TWO RULEBOOKS — the EU governs it as HARD LAW (binding, fineable; EU AI Act Art. 15 / OBL-EUAI-015) while India governs it by SELF-CERTIFICATION (advisory, principle-based; RBI FREE-AI / OBL-RBI-001), modalities read from the obligation graph and India framed with respect (a deliberate, innovation-preserving choice and the harder engineering problem, never 'behind'); (2) ONE REPORT, EVERY REGULATOR — the same signed STR rendered into FINnet (FIU-IND) AND goAML (UN, 70+ countries), one fact model two formats. Inherits the KS-0501 design system (keystone.ui.tokens + keystone.ui.svg); custom SVG embedded via components.v1.html, live + replay; ships its own AppTest gate.
done_criteria: the screen renders the EU hard-law vs India self-certification contrast (modalities from the obligation graph) and the same STR in both FINnet and goAML, all from the run-result (live + replay), inheriting the KS-0501 design tokens
India's framework is framed with respect (harder engineering problem, deliberate choice), never as deficient
an AppTest loads the running app (live + replay) and asserts it renders without exception

features.title: Supporting shell — ledger, posture, assurance before/after
features.description: The navigable frame around the two hero screens: a sidebar shell (keystone.ui.shell_app) that HOSTS the existing seam + jurisdiction heroes (imported, not reimplemented) and adds three supporting views built on keystone.ui.svg — the hash-chained evidence ledger (the auditable-by-construction arc), the cross-layer posture (L3 obligations / L2 assurance / L1 fraud at a glance), and the assurance before/after (Garak 10/12 → 0/12 after the Guardrails patch, REFERENCED via the canonical RunResult v3 assurance block). Quiet by design (the heroes carry the thesis). Inherits the KS-0501 design system; embedded via components.v1.html, live + replay; ships its own AppTest gate.
done_criteria: a single shell hosts both heroes (not reimplemented) and the three supporting views, navigable, rendering from the run-result (live + replay) in the shared design system
the ledger view shows the verifiable arc + chain, posture shows all three layers, and the before/after shows the real referenced 10/12 → 0/12 — all from the run-result
an AppTest loads the running shell and cycles every view (live + replay) without exception

features.title: Recorded-run fallback (offline demo path)
features.description: The demo safety net: a single committed, genuinely-produced v3 recorded run (src/keystone/demo/recorded_run.json, exposed via keystone.demo.recorded_run_path / load_recorded_run — one source of truth) the shell replays fully offline, indistinguishable from a live run. Recorded is the shell's SAFE DEFAULT (instant, deterministic, zero network/Ollama/GPU). Proven: the replay path renders all five views with sockets blocked. Hardening over the version-aware loader + replay KS-0500/0502 enabled; no new capability.
done_criteria: a committed, genuinely-produced v3 recorded run replays through the real UI and renders all five views with NO network/Ollama (proven with sockets blocked)
the recorded run is version-clean, hash-chain valid offline, round-trips, and matches a fresh build on its substantive values (not hand-edited)
the recorded run is the shell's safe default data source (works if you forget to flip it)

features.title: Demo script + rehearsal (the narrated walkthrough)
features.description: The end-to-end demo script tying the screens into a narrated walkthrough (seam hero → jurisdiction hero → supporting shell) with a rehearsal pass. The gated place where a static-vs-animated reveal call is made if rehearsal needs it (animation stays out of the per-screen builds).
done_criteria: a documented demo script drives the full walkthrough across the screens from a single run, rehearsed end to end

features.title: Seam Framework abstraction (M1-01) — bind any (attack, crime) pair uniformly
features.description: Generalises the single TXN-000016 seam (KS-0403) into a framework that binds ANY (OWASP LLM attack class, FATF financial-crime typology) pair under ONE uniform independence guarantee and ONE uniform build-failing drift assertion (keystone.assurance.framework). A pair is attack side (OWASP id + canonical VulnerabilitySignature + channel) × crime side (FATF typology + memo-blind detector) × a binding on a shared transaction id × a result classification (CLEAN / BOUNDARY / OPEN). The independence guarantee is a framework PROPERTY, not a per-pair test: bind() only ever hands the crime detector a FinancialProjection (the event with every attack channel stripped), and the detector is typed to accept that wrapper and nothing else, so it structurally cannot read the attack-bearing field. P1 (prompt-injection × structuring) is re-expressed as the framework's FIRST instance (P1_PAIR) and binds through bind(); ALL existing seam tests pass unchanged — the faithfulness proof. BOUNDARY (a pair that provably does NOT bind — P4) and OPEN (report-as-found — P5) are first-class results, not errors. Architecture only — no new pair, no RunResult/UI change; lives on the edge (import-linter KEPT, core stays unaware of attacks).
done_criteria: all existing seam tests pass with P1 expressed THROUGH the framework (P1_PAIR bound by bind()) — the faithfulness proof; no existing assertion weakened

the uniform independence guarantee is enforced framework-level: the crime detector only ever receives the financial projection (FinancialProjection), never the attack-bearing channel, asserted over every registered pair
the build-failing drift assertion fires when a CLEAN pair's two sides are forced to disagree, and a BOUNDARY pair's 'no typology fires' negative is representable and proven by test

features.title: P2 — Prompt Injection × Rapid-movement/layering (M1-02)
features.description: The seam matrix's second pair through the UNCHANGED M1-01 framework: a memo-borne prompt injection (CANONICAL_FORWARDING_EXPLOIT -> MEMO_FORWARDING_SIGNATURE) whose instruction drives FAST onward fan-out, caught independently by the FATF rapid-movement/layering typology (RAPID_MOVEMENT) on velocity + fan-out alone — the AML side never reads the memo (inherited FinancialProjection independence). P2_PAIR (keystone.assurance.seam_p2) mirrors P1 and registers alongside it in REGISTERED_PAIRS, inheriting bind()'s three rigor mechanisms — no new framework machinery. Its substrate is a new seeded core generator (rapid_movement_cluster / rapid_sample_stream): small (<5k) fast fan-out to distinct recipients — additive, so P1's sample_stream stays byte-identical. Anti-collapse (M1-00 §6): P2's pattern fires RAPID_MOVEMENT and NOT STRUCTURING (sub-band), detector-distinct from P1's structuring cluster. Core+assurance only; no schema/UI change. Axis-A breadth (same attack class as P1, a new typology).
done_criteria: P2 binds CLEAN through the UNCHANGED framework: the SAME transaction id carries the P2 forwarding signature AND is independently caught as RAPID_MOVEMENT (memo-blind) — the strong shared-transaction-id assertion mirrored from P1

the anti-collapse distinctness guard holds: P2's pattern fires RAPID_MOVEMENT and NOT STRUCTURING (a visibly different shape from P1's sub-threshold structuring cluster), and the two pairs bind to distinct typologies
P2 inherits the framework's independence property and build-failing drift assertion (forcing P2's two sides to disagree raises SeamDriftError); P1 and all prior tests stay green, untouched

features.title: P3 — Prompt Injection × Large-transfer/threshold (M1-03)
features.description: The seam matrix's third pair through the UNCHANGED M1-01 framework, completing Axis A (one attack class — OWASP LLM01 — manifesting as THREE distinct FATF typologies: small-many / fast-onward / single-large). A memo-borne prompt injection (CANONICAL_LARGE_TRANSFER_EXPLOIT -> MEMO_LARGE_TRANSFER_SIGNATURE) instructs a SINGLE threshold-breaching transfer, caught independently by the FATF large-transfer/threshold typology (LARGE_TRANSFER) on AMOUNT alone — the AML side never reads the memo (inherited FinancialProjection independence). P3_PAIR (keystone.assurance.seam_p3) mirrors P1/P2 and registers in REGISTERED_PAIRS, inheriting bind()'s three rigor mechanisms — no new framework machinery. Substrate: a new seeded core generator (large_transfer / large_sample_stream) emitting one transfer in [14k,21k] over the 10k CTR threshold; additive, so P1/P2 streams stay byte-identical. P3 is the cleanly-EXCLUSIVE pair: a single transfer fires LARGE_TRANSFER and NEITHER structuring (needs >=3 in-band) nor rapid-movement (needs >=5 velocity). Core+assurance only; no schema/UI change.
done_criteria: P3 binds CLEAN through the UNCHANGED framework: the SAME transaction id carries the P3 large-transfer signature AND is independently caught as LARGE_TRANSFER (memo-blind, on amount) — the strong shared-transaction-id assertion mirrored from P1/P2
the distinctness guard holds in full: P3's pattern fires LARGE_TRANSFER and NEITHER structuring nor rapid-movement (the cleanly-exclusive single-typology pair, no overlap caveat)
P3 inherits the framework's independence property and build-failing drift assertion; P1, P2, and all prior tests stay green, untouched

features.title: P4 — Sensitive Information Disclosure × (none) — THE BOUNDARY (M1-04)
features.description: The characterized BOUNDARY of the seam matrix (Axis B; OWASP LLM06, the 'Vault Whisper' exfil class, cf. arXiv:2601.22569) — a PROVEN NEGATIVE, the paper's credibility anchor. An exfiltration injection (EXFIL_INJECTION_SIGNATURE, outcome DATA_DISCLOSURE) LEAKS another party's data and moves NO money, so the event's financial footprint is empty and NO transaction-monitoring typology can fire — the seam provably does NOT bind. P4_PAIR (keystone.assurance.seam_p4) is a result=BOUNDARY pair through the framework's existing boundary support (crime.typology=None; bind() asserts ZERO typologies fire and returns an all-None binding). The negative is PRINCIPLED, not incidental: it is a property of the attack (a data-disclosure outcome produces no transaction → nothing for any typology to act on), proven by running the SAME full detect suite that fires on P1/P2/P3 and showing it fires zero on P4. Build-protected: if exfil ever moved money (a typology fires), bind() raises SeamDriftError. No forced positive (M1-00 §4 forbids it); no new framework/typology, no schema/UI change.
done_criteria: P4 registers as a result=BOUNDARY pair and binds as a PROVEN NEGATIVE through the unchanged framework: the exfil attack signature resolves (the attack is real and present), AND zero typologies fire on its financial projection, AND bind() returns an all-None boundary binding

Core stays attack-unaware; no schema/UI change.

features.title: The Evidence Model — seam event AS compliance audit evidence (M2-01)
features.description: Movement 2's architectural core (the analog of M1-01's framework): a typed EvidenceRelationship (keystone.convergence) binding a seam event to a named, EXISTING Layer-3 obligation, carrying the M2-00 §2 four-part rigor AS STRUCTURE so an inadmissible mapping cannot be constructed — (1) obligation (ObligationRef.from_obligation, built FROM the real core.obligations graph by id, NOT a parallel registry — subsumes L3), (2) requirement (the real control text), (3) reason (MANDATORY, non-empty — the anti-checklist guard, enforced at construction), and (4) a SATISFY/VIOLATE state DERIVED from the before/after assurance data (derive_state: VIOLATE while the attack succeeds, SATISFY once detected+blocked — a pure function of the numbers, never a static flag). The relationship carries BeforeAfter (built FROM REFERENCED_ASSURANCE 10->0, can't drift) and exposes BOTH pre_state (VIOLATE) + post_state (SATISFY) + the temporal transition. The boundary (EvidenceKind.NOT_EVIDENCED — 'this event does NOT evidence this obligation', reason still mandatory) is first-class, mirroring M1's BOUNDARY. The model names itself as defensible technical-compliance EVIDENCE REASONING, NOT a legal/certified verdict (EVIDENCE_DISCLAIMER, M2-00 §6). Proven by ONE reference mapping (P1 memo-injection × EU AI Act Art. 15, OBL-EUAI-015 / CTL-ROBUST-01 — VIOLATE->SATISFY); the other mappings are M2-02+. Edge logic over L3 + the matrix; the core stays unaware (import-linter KEPT). L3 untouched.
done_criteria: the reference mapping constructs with all four parts (real cited obligation + requirement grounded in the control text + a specific mandatory reason + a satisfy/violate state), and SUBSUMES the existing L3 obligation (ObligationRef built from the loaded Obligation, not a parallel registry)

features.title: The Red-Team Agent — adaptive offensive policy (MA-01); Keystone's first genuine agent
features.description: The first component that meets the strict agent bar (MA-00 §2): keystone.agents.red_team observes the outcome of each prompt-injection probe it fires and CHOOSES its next probe to exploit what is getting through — escalating within the family whose attacks land, abandoning families the defense fully blocks. The decision space is REAL: the 23 in-family prompt-injection probes Garak v0.15.1 ships across the two recognized families (latentinjection ×17, promptinject ×6; enumerated live from `garak --list_probes`, selectable via ScanConfig.probes/--probes), ≥2 families and ≥2 probes/family so 'choose' is meaningful. The choice at step N is a function of the outcomes of steps 1..N-1 (choose_next) — so it is an agent, not a for-loop. HONEST framing (MA-00 §3): this is Option B — an adaptive offensive POLICY, named as such (MECHANISM = 'adaptive offensive policy … not an LLM'), NOT claimed as an LLM agent (Option A is a later upgrade). This makes keystone.agents' forward-promise TRUE: one genuine agent now lives there. Does NOT add the Triage Agent (MB) and does NOT re-derive the matrix (Movement 1 stays the characterized result).

THE honesty test (the proof of agency): the SAME failure_rate routes DIFFERENTLY by seam context — at a moderate above-floor rate (MEDIUM severity), CLEAN → remediate, BOUNDARY → accept, OPEN → escalate; the route is a function of the COMBINATION, not an `if rate > X`. The 3-option action space is genuine (a realistic finding routes to each — no 2-of-3-dead). HONEST framing (MB-00 §3): this is Option B — an adaptive triage POLICY, named as such (MECHANISM = 'adaptive triage policy … not an LLM'), NOT claimed as an LLM agent (Option A is a later upgrade). SCOPE honesty (§1/§6): 'remediate' is a ROUTE (this finding warrants remediation), NOT a Defense Agent choosing among fixes — fix-selection / the adversarial loop is gated Movement C. Record/replay (MB-00 §4): schema v6→v7 — the routing decision lands on RunResult.triage (TriageView), DERIVED by actually running the agent (demo.triage.build_triage_view) over signals the runner hands it; the supervisor-over-worker topology is literal (the failure_rate it reads IS the MA-01 Red-Team Agent's strongest landed exploit); recorded_run.json regenerated as a genuine v7 run; recorded==fresh holds; all replay paths green; the hash chain re-verifies. The memo-blind boundary (MB-00 §4, sacred): the supervisor reads ONLY already-computed signals as plain values and imports NOTHING on the detection path or the attack channel (it carries its OWN value enums, parity-locked to the originals); an AST import-scan asserts it; the four independence locks hold with BOTH agents present. With MA-01 this makes Keystone honestly MULTI-AGENT: an offensive worker (Red-Team) + a supervisor (Triage), a supervisor-worker topology, verifiable in code. Does NOT make the defense agentic and does NOT re-derive the matrix.

features.title: The live Triage Agent — genuine LLM-reasoned routing, opt-in, policy fallback (OPT-A-01)
features.description: Takes the Triage Agent (KS-0613) from a transparent policy to genuine LLM reasoning as a STRICTLY ADDITIVE, opt-in live mode (OPT-A-00 the design contract). keystone.agents.triage gains live_triage / triage_live: a local LLM (qwen2.5:3b via Ollama) is prompted with the finding's SIGNALS ONLY (failure_rate, seam_result, severity) and the three allowed routes, and asked to pick EXACTLY ONE + a one-line rationale — bounded selection, not open planning (OPT-A-00 §7.3). Reuses the ONE allowed inference seam (keystone.llm.inference.complete, ADR-0008) — no new client. features.title: Triage LLM prompt-rescue — a genuine prompt effort re-tests the OPT-A-01 3B finding (model or prompt?)
features.description: Revisits KS-0616 / ADR-0021's honest-negative 3B finding (qwen2.5:3b agreed with the triage policy on only 1/6 scenarios, collapsed to one route, misread the numeric failure_rate) by asking whether that was a WEAK PROMPT or a weak model. Makes a genuine prompt-engineering effort — the ONLY thing that changes is keystone.agents.triage's LLM prompt (TRIAGE_SYSTEM + build_live_prompt), applying four levers that target the specific OPT-A-01 failures: (1) signal clarity — failure_rate presented with its 0.00–1.00 scale + explicit percent so the number cannot be misread; (2) the task taught in words — the policy's decision rules in order (HIGH→escalate; <0.10→accept; then seam open/boundary/clean→escalate/accept/remediate) with the interplay stated; (3) five few-shot worked examples (signal→route→why) covering all three routes + the interplay + the HIGH override, at HELD-OUT rates (0.65/0.05/0.90) that never equal an eval tuple and each verified against route_for by a mechanical test; (4) structured output (ROUTE|WHY). The memo-blind boundary (OPT-A-00 §4, sacred) HOLDS — the prompt carries the three SIGNALS only, few-shot examples are signal→route pairs never attack text, and the AST import-scan boundary test still passes. The policy (route_for, ground truth + fallback + eval baseline), the fallback, reasoner-tagging, the schema, and the opt-in default are ALL unchanged — the policy stays the default. The evaluation (scripts/triage_llm_eval.py / make triage-eval) gains a second, permanent block: a HELD-OUT anti-parrot probe of six novel (seam,severity)+rate combos the examples never show, each built so a model that PARROTS the examples gets it WRONG while one applying the override RULES gets it right — separating genuine rule-application from few-shot pattern-matching. Honest before/after: in-distribution agreement rose 1/6 → 6/6 (stable, 30/30 at 5×; no collapse, reads the rate, honors interplay) BUT the held-out probe stayed at 4/6 (12/18) with the numeric misread resurfacing (called 0.06 'above 0.10') and seam semantics misapplied (called a clean seam 'contained'). Verdict = both at once: the OPT-A-01 finding was PART prompt artifact AND the model ceiling is real — 3B pattern-matches the examples, it does not robustly apply the rules; the policy stays the default, now better-evidenced (ADR-0026). Does NOT promote LLM routing to the default (a separate, joint decision this result does not justify), does NOT change the policy / fallback / tagging / boundary / schema / arc, and does NOT escalate to an 8B model (3B only).
done_criteria: the ONLY code change is the triage LLM prompt (TRIAGE_SYSTEM + build_live_prompt) applying the four levers; the policy route_for, the fallback, reasoner-tagging, the schema, and the opt-in default are unchanged — the policy stays the default reasoner

every few-shot worked example baked into the prompt matches route_for (a mechanical test pins this) and uses (rate, seam, severity) tuples held out from every eval scenario — the eval tests generalization, not memorization

the memo-blind boundary holds: the prompt carries the three signals only (no memo / attack channel / detector), few-shot examples are signal→route pairs not attack text, and the AST import-scan boundary test still passes; the constrained-output + reasoner-honesty + fallback tests pass unchanged

the SAME evaluation is re-run for a fair before/after and reported truthfully, not cherry-picked: in-distribution 1/6 → 6/6, plus a held-out anti-parrot probe (4/6) that the eval now prints permanently as a distinct block

the honest verdict is recorded (ADR-0026, OPEN_QUESTIONS Finding 1): part prompt artifact, part model ceiling; the policy stays the default and the promotion decision is deferred to a joint call; make check / make verify green, mypy strict / Ruff / import-linter clean with no new ignores, all prior tests green unchanged

features.title: Live scan-scoping + granular --live flags — bounded default red-team scan, one agent live without the other (OPT-A-02b)
features.description: Fixes the two operational pains OPT-A-02/OPT-A-01b surfaced: (1) a full live Garak sequence is intractable (LatentWhois ~1550s, the *Full variants ~955s+, one >1800s timeout, the whole sequence HOURS), and (2) `keystone demo --live` drove BOTH agents from ONE flag, so asking for live triage (a ~13s LLM call) launched the hours-long red-team scan too. PART 1 — smart scan scoping: keystone.agents.red_team classifies DEEP_PROBES (the *Full variants + LatentWhois) from the REAL OPT-A-02 timings ONLY (grounded in _OPT_A_02_CAPTURES prompt counts — LatentWhois 168, EnFrFull 270, vs ~12-24 for the leads — and ADR-0025 / OPEN_QUESTIONS Finding 2 durations; nothing invented); tractable_catalog() is PROBE_CATALOG minus the deep probes; live_red_team gains scope (SCOPE_TRACTABLE default / SCOPE_FULL) which hands the UNCHANGED choose_next policy a scoped CATALOG — the default live red-team scans only the tractable set (real, but bounded to minutes), with an opt-in --deep for the full set incl. the monsters (hours). This is scan SCOPING, not a logic change: the policy's selection LOGIC and full capability are untouched (a --deep run exercises the whole space), a scoped-out probe is not-run (never a fabricated 'scanned' result), and every RedTeamTrace / RedTeamView records scan_scope (tractable/full) so a reader knows whether the deep probes were included. PART 2 — granular flags: --live-triage (LLM triage only; red-team stays recorded_profile — NO Garak scan, so live triage never triggers the hours-long scan, the OPT-A-01b pain fixed and pinned by a test), --live-redteam (real Garak, tractable by default; + --deep for the full set), and --live (both live, red-team tractable — redefined from the old 'both, full/hours'). The runner threads a LiveModes(triage, redteam, deep) bundle; the DEFAULT (no flags) stays FULLY OFFLINE (the data-residency front door, works with no Garak/Ollama). Schema: NO version bump — RedTeamView.scan_scope defaults to 'full' (a run recorded before scoping had the whole catalog available, so old data still loads truthfully); recorded_run.json regenerated offline (recorded==fresh). The memo-blind boundary (sacred) HOLDS and the AST import-scan still passes. Does NOT change any agent's decision logic (the red-team choose_next policy, triage routing), the fallback mechanism, the source/reasoner tagging semantics, the schema meaning, or make any live mode the default. Real proof: `keystone demo --live-triage` runs in ~13s (real LLM route, red-team recorded_profile, no scan) where before it was 26+ minutes; `keystone demo --live-redteam` is a real tractable scan (garak_live, scan_scope=tractable, no monster probe); `--deep` opts into the full set.
done_criteria: the deep/intractable probes are classified from the REAL OPT-A-02 timings only (DEEP_PROBES = the *Full variants + LatentWhois, grounded in _OPT_A_02_CAPTURES prompt counts + ADR-0025 durations) — nothing invented; tractable_catalog() is the full catalog minus those

the DEFAULT live red-team scans only the tractable set (scope=tractable — no monster probe is ever selected, so a real scan is bounded to minutes); an explicit --deep / SCOPE_FULL run exercises the FULL catalog incl. the deep probes; the trace/view record scan_scope so a reader knows which ran

scoping does NOT change the agent's selection LOGIC or space: the scoped catalog is handed to the unchanged choose_next policy, the full catalog is intact, a scoped-out probe is not-run (never a fabricated 'scanned' result), and the §2 agency + full-sequence (--deep) behaviour still hold

granular flags control each agent's live mode independently: --live-triage runs LLM triage with NO red-team scan (the OPT-A-01b pain, pinned by a test asserting red_team.source == recorded_profile), --live-redteam runs a real tractable scan (+ --deep for full), --live runs both (red-team tractable); the default (no flags) stays fully offline and needs no Garak/Ollama

no agent decision logic / the fallback mechanism / the source+reasoner tagging semantics / the memo-blind boundary / the schema meaning changed; NO schema bump (RedTeamView.scan_scope defaults to 'full'); recorded_run.json regenerated offline (recorded==fresh, hash chain re-verifies); the AST import-scan boundary test still passes; make check / make verify green, mypy strict / Ruff / import-linter clean with no new ignores; all prior tests green

features.title: Remediation (c) — a genuine, distinct financial-side remediation (the 2nd menu option that unblocks Movement C)
features.description: MC-PRE-01. The remediation probe (remediation_probe.md) returned MENU-FIRST: Movement C (a defense agent + adversarial loop) is honest only if a defender chooses among >=2 genuinely-distinct remediations, but only ONE existed (the AI-side NeMo Guardrails input rail, loop.CONTROL_NAME). This builds the SECOND, genuinely-distinct remediation — (c) financial-side detection tightening — as REAL capability, and PROVES it distinct via missed-then-caught. Form chosen: a stricter FatfThresholds PROFILE (keystone.core.fatf.STRICT_THRESHOLDS: CTR 10k->5k, structuring band floor 5k->2.5k in step so the band stays valid) applied through a new keystone.assurance.remediation module — chosen over the flagged-destinations form because FatfThresholds is ALREADY a detect() parameter (the seam is built for exactly this: zero new plumbing, pure tightening of existing rules, not new list data). The module catalogs the menu (REMEDIATION_MENU = two Remediation entries, one per SeamSide {AI, FINANCIAL}) and exposes (c) as callables: tighten_financial_detection(stream) = detect(stream, STRICT_THRESHOLDS), and newly_flagged_by_tightening(stream) = findings strict catches that baseline misses. (c) acts on the OPPOSITE side of the L2<->L1 seam from (a): (a) blocks the AI/prompt injection, (c) flags the money movement — different layer, different outcome, a finding-dependent choice. Memo-blind boundary SACRED and preserved: (c) re-runs the SAME memo-blind detect() engine (never reads Transaction.memo), a test pins detect(strict) blank == injected; the AST import-scan / import-linter contracts pass (assurance -> core allowed; core imports nothing new; core stays edge-free). NO schema bump (reuses Finding/detect; STRICT_THRESHOLDS is additive core data; DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS and baseline behaviour byte-unchanged). Does NOT build a defense AGENT (that is MC-01, deliberately after the menu — the Remediation entries are a descriptive catalog, not a uniform callable interface yet), does NOT touch (a)/the guardrail rail behaviour, the agents' decision logic, the seam, or the ledger.
done_criteria: remediation (c) is a REAL, callable financial-side remediation (keystone.assurance.remediation.tighten_financial_detection re-runs the memo-blind detect() at core.fatf.STRICT_THRESHOLDS) — a distinct action parallel to the guardrail patch, not a cosmetic knob

THE PROOF missed-then-caught: a lone 9,000 transfer (just under the standard 10k CTR) is flagged by NEITHER baseline typology (not a >=3 structuring cluster, not a >=10k large transfer, not a flagged recipient) but IS flagged as LARGE_TRANSFER under (c) — same transaction, opposite outcome, driven only by (c); newly_flagged_by_tightening returns exactly that finding

(c) is genuinely distinct from (a): it acts on the FINANCIAL side of the seam (flag the money) vs (a) on the AI side (block the prompt) — REMEDIATION_MENU holds 2 entries on opposite SeamSides with distinct control names; GUARDRAIL_PATCH_CONTROL == loop.CONTROL_NAME (a is referenced, unchanged)

memo-blind boundary holds with (c) present: (c) re-runs the same memo-blind engine (detect(strict) identical for blank vs injected memo), the AST import-scan boundary test and import-linter contracts still pass; DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS and baseline detection are byte-unchanged; NO schema bump

features.title: The Defense Agent (MC-01) — a third genuine agent choosing the remediation (a vs c), policy-first, proven flip
features.description: MC-01. Builds Keystone's THIRD genuine agent, gated by a built-in Phase-0 probe that PROVES the choice is finding-dependent before any build (else it is theater). Phase 0 (passed): findings carry INDEPENDENT two-sided strength — the AI-side failure_rate (the Red-Team's landed-exploit rate, a Garak scan of model susceptibility) and the financial-side financial_gap (does a transaction slip baseline FATF detection but get caught once tightened — keystone.assurance.remediation.financial_detection_gap, memo-blind) — measured by different subsystems over different aspects, NOT correlated (the real demo finding is failure_rate 0.92 with financial_gap False; the lone-9,000 tx is low-rate with financial_gap True), so the choice can genuinely flip. Phase 1 — the uniform interface (MC-00 §2): each Remediation implements apply(context) -> RemediationOutcome; the agent selects on the finding then dispatches uniformly (never a signature-branch); the outcome keeps `side` (never erases the seam difference) and is honestly asymmetric — verified_offline True/False for (c) (an offline detection change, verifiable now) or None for (a) (an AI-path control whose effect needs the MC-02 re-scan); retest_via is the loop-ready handle for MC-02. Phase 2 — the agent (MC-00 §3): keystone.agents.defense.defend reads DefenseSignals (memo-blind) and chooses via a TRANSPARENT POLICY, NOT an LLM (OPT-A-01b evidence: 3B can't reason a bounded choice reliably; LLM-reasoned choice is compute-gated; honest label 'adaptive defense policy … not an LLM'). Both signals matter: (c) iff (financial_gap and failure_rate < 0.10), else (a) — the money-side is chosen only when money is provably slipping detection AND the injection is contained; otherwise the AI rail (root-cause control when the injection is live, structural default otherwise). The decision (chosen remediation + why + applied outcome) is recorded on RunResult.defense. Schema: NO version bump — `defense` is an OPTIONAL defaulted (None) field on RunResult (old runs still load; mirrors OPT-A-01's reasoner / OPT-A-02's source); recorded_run.json regenerated offline (recorded==fresh). The memo-blind boundary (MC-00 §4, sacred) HOLDS with all three agents present: the CHOICE reads signals only, and even APPLYING (c) uses the memo-blind detector (a test pins detect(strict) blank == injected); the AST import-scan boundary test and import-linter contracts pass (the Defense Agent dispatches remediations so it imports assurance.remediation — allowed — but reaches no attack channel / detector-lock directly, pinned by a defense-specific boundary test). Does NOT close the adversarial loop / re-scan (that is MC-02 — the retest_via handle is built loop-ready, not wired), does NOT LLM-reason the choice (policy-first), does NOT change the existing agents' logic / (a)/(c)'s core behaviour / the seam / the FATF baseline / the ledger, and makes no autonomous-self-healing claim (the agent chooses; humans govern). Keystone is now a three-agent system on both sides of the seam (Red-Team offense, Triage supervisor, Defense defender).
done_criteria: the Phase-0 gate is proven from real data: findings carry independent two-sided strength (AI-side failure_rate + memo-blind financial_gap) that is not always correlated, so the (a)-vs-(c) choice can genuinely flip — documented in the PR as a probe block, and if it could NOT flip the task stops (honest gate)

THE FLIP: a strong-AI/weak-financial finding chooses (a) guardrail block, a weak-AI/strong-financial finding chooses (c) money-side tighten — same finding same choice, both remediations reachable, the choice a pure function of the finding (tests/test_defense_agent.py)

the uniform interface: (a) and (c) both implement apply(context) -> RemediationOutcome and the agent dispatches uniformly WITHOUT erasing the seam-side difference (side on every outcome) or leaking the signature into the choice; verified_offline is honestly asymmetric (bool for (c) now, None for (a) — verified by the MC-02 re-scan); retest_via built loop-ready

policy-first (NOT an LLM): the reasoner tag is always 'policy'; the decision is recorded on RunResult.defense; the offline / data-residency default is intact (the choice needs no model); NO schema bump (defense is an optional defaulted field); recorded_run.json regenerated offline (recorded==fresh, hash chain re-verifies)

the memo-blind boundary holds with all THREE agents present: the choice reads signals only, applying (c) is memo-blind (detect(strict) blank == injected), the AST import-scan boundary test and import-linter contracts pass; MC-01 STOPS at applying the remediation (no loop / re-scan); make check / make verify green, mypy strict / Ruff / import-linter clean with no new ignores, all prior tests green unchanged
